sections.heading: The Spanish interference problem
sections.body: Spanish uses preterite for both functions. 'He visto esa película' can mean both 'I saw that film' (specific past event) and 'I have seen that film' (life experience). English separates these into two tenses with strict rules.

sections.heading: Present Perfect: connection to now
sections.body: Use Present Perfect when the time of the action is unspecified, irrelevant, or still connected to the present. Three main uses: experience ('I've been to Japan'), recent past with present result ('She's broken her arm'), and continuing situations ('I've worked here for 5 years').

sections.heading: Past Simple: completed in the past
sections.body: Use Past Simple when the action is complete and the time is specified or implied to be a finished period: 'yesterday', 'last week', 'in 2019', 'when I was a child', 'after dinner'.

examples.english: I saw that film last Friday.

examples.note: Recent past with present result: she still doesn't have them

items: I ___ (never / try) sushi. Do you recommend it?

quiz.question: Which sentence is correct?
quiz.explanation: 'Ever' in a general life-experience question uses Present Perfect: 'Have you ever eaten...?'
